title: Contact

title: Research
text: My curiosity and research interest sit between how climate extremes transition to environmental changes and variability, particularly water quality and ecological health. My current projects examine drought-algal bloom dynamics and how drought influences physicochemical properties of water and shape biological indicators of river health.

[4] Hemie Cho, Pamela Sofia Fabian, Sang-Hoon Lee, Kyung-A You, Hyun-Han Kwon, ‘Decadal Trends and Predictive Insights into Aquatic Ecosystem Health in the Namhan River, South Korea: Longitudinal Analysis of Hydro-Environmental Factors and Stream Health Indices (2008–2022)’, Environmental Pollution 2024. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.envpol.2024.125184
[3] Pamela Sofia Fabian, Hyun-Han Kwon, Meththika Vithanage, Joo-Heon Lee, ‘Modeling, challenges and strategies for understanding impacts of climate extremes (droughts and floods) on water quality in Asia: A review’, Environmental Research 2023. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.envres.2023.115617
[2] Pamela Sofia Fabian, Dong Hoon Lee, Sang Wook Shin, and Joo-Hyon Kang, ‘Assessment of Pyrene Adsorption on Biochars Prepared from Green Infrastructure Plants: Toward a Closed-Loop Recycling in Managing Toxic Stormwater Pollutants’, Journal of Water Process Engineering 2022. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jwpe.2022.102929

title: About
text: I’m a Research Professor at University of Seoul. My research focuses on hydrology, particularly climate extremes and their relation to freshwater ecology. I work on understanding extreme events impacts to our water resources and how we can adapt. I am interested in the space where environmental science, policy, and industry meet, and in building research that is legible and useful across all three.
